explainer: Each row is a dog-whistle root that lives in the grouped-split TEST set — Run A's training data never contained this root, so any RQB Run A prediction here is a cold-start generalisation. `in_alt_train` flags whether the same root was in the alt-split (ingroup-stratified) TRAIN data, which is what gave Run C the chance to memorise the term→ingroup glossary mapping. The 0.996 macro-F1 of Run C is built on this leak.
